$comment: v0.12.12 initial 12-pair labeled set for the Coach-Player benchmark. 4 grounded (answer fully supported by facts), 4 partial (mostly supported, 1-2 unsupported claims), 4 hallucinated (major claims not supported by any fact). Post-launch target: expand to 30+.

pairs.answer: POST /users requires a JWT bearer token.

facts.fact_text: POST /users requires JWT authentication in the Authorization header

pairs.query: What is the retry policy for the payment webhook?
pairs.answer: The payment webhook retries three times with exponential backoff starting at one second.

facts.fact_text: Payment webhook retries 3 times with exponential backoff starting at 1s

pairs.query: Which database does the session store use?
pairs.answer: Sessions are stored in Redis with a 24-hour expiry.

facts.fact_text: Session store backed by Redis
Session expiry is 24 hours

pairs.query: How is user email uniqueness enforced?
pairs.answer: Email uniqueness is enforced at the database layer via a unique index on the email column of the users table.

facts.fact_text: users table has UNIQUE index on email column

pairs.answer: POST /users requires JWT authentication, returns 201 on success, validates email format server-side, and is rate-limited to sixty requests per minute.

facts.fact_text: POST /users requires JWT authentication
POST /users returns 201 on success
POST /users validates email format server-side using RFC 5322

pairs.answer: The auth middleware validates the JWT signature, extracts the user ID from the sub claim, loads the user record from the database, and refreshes the token if it expires within five minutes.

facts.fact_text: Auth middleware validates JWT signature using RS256
User ID is extracted from the JWT sub claim
Auth middleware loads user record from Postgres

pairs.answer: Deploys use GitHub Actions to build a Docker image, push to ECR, then update the ECS service via Terraform apply. Rollbacks are automatic on 5xx spike.

facts.fact_text: GitHub Actions workflow builds a Docker image on push to main
Docker image is pushed to AWS ECR
ECS service update runs via Terraform apply

pairs.query: How does the search API rank results?
pairs.answer: Search results are ranked by BM25 score, boosted by recency, filtered by user access permissions, and re-ranked using a neural model trained on click-through data.

facts.fact_text: Search ranking uses BM25 scoring on the primary text index
Search results are boosted by recency (last-modified date)
Search filters results by user access permissions before ranking

pairs.$note: 0 of 3 claims verified — Coach must not invent support
pairs.answer: POST /users uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, tokens signed by HS512, and rotates keys every 90 days.

facts.fact_text: POST /users requires JWT authentication in the Authorization header

pairs.$note: Answer references an endpoint that isn't in the facts at all
pairs.answer: The /admin/reset-passwords endpoint accepts a batch of user IDs, generates temporary passwords, emails them via SES, and logs the operation to Splunk.

facts.fact_text: POST /users requires JWT authentication
GET /users/{id} returns a user profile

pairs.query: Which database does the session store use?
pairs.answer: Sessions are stored in DynamoDB with a global secondary index on user_id and a 7-day TTL managed by Lambda triggers.

facts.fact_text: Session store backed by Redis
Session expiry is 24 hours

pairs.$note: Confident-sounding but every technical claim is invented
pairs.answer: The cache layer uses a two-tier design: L1 is an in-process LRU with a 50MB budget, L2 is a Redis cluster with consistent hashing across three shards, and cache entries are invalidated by a change-data-capture stream from Postgres.

facts.fact_text: Rate limiting uses a token bucket with 60 requests per minute
